title: Age of Empires III: Complete
notes:
Entering the CD-Key is impossible, unless "corefonts" are installed (The default Font's character width is too large to fit into the Textboxes).
Installing mfc42 is necessary, otherwise the game will throw up a "PID DLL missing" error when attempting to verify the entered CD-Key.

The game starts up, Intro Cutscenes play without any issues. However, instead of showing the Main Menu Screen, the Game crashes.
Launching the Game in a Virtual Desktop results in an infinite loading screen with misplaced textures instead of a crash message.

title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

gpu: Intel Open Source Technology Center Mesa DRI Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (Broadwell GT2) x86/MMX/SSE2
notes: Game works with wine if you pick the Windows XP preset on .cfg
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
You have to set "Windows Version" to "Windows XP" in winecfg and install l3codecx with winetricks. That's enough to make "Asian Dynasties" (and  "The War Chiefs", but that's less important) playable. I didn't play game much, but as far as I can tell you have access to all content (campaigns, faction, etc.) from main game and "The War Chiefs" expansion  in "Asian Dynasties", so you don't really need to launch main game ("Play Age of Empires III: Complete Collection" in menu), which required key. If for some reason you want to launch it, you have to install mfc42 and (maybe, I don't have to) corefonts with winetricks to make key insert dialog work.

On Debian (and probably on derivatives) Proton prefixes located in the "$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata" directory, so you can do this to launch winetricks for "Age of Empires III":

export WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx"
winetricks --gui
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 480 Graphics (POLARIS10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.8-2-MANJARO, LLVM 6.0.1)
notes: After running 'protontricks 105450 mfc42' to get rid of the dll issue with the license key, and ensuring I had corefonts installed, I can get through the opening movies but then it crashes when it tries to load the menu.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
I managed to get it to run by following the steps in this post: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/17#issuecomment-423592026

I had to edit the path to the steamapps folder location in the post linked to above and in the protontricks Python script, because I've changed the location of the Steam Library folder using the Steam client and it's different from the default location.

There were also a lot of errors and warnings thrown at me as I was installing the required components, but it apparently didn't matter since I was able to play a multi-player game session that lasted for more than an hour and it ran without any noticeable issues.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
UNOFFICIAL

rm -r "${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450"

WINEPREFIX="${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/" winetricks d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts

title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: Unable to put in CD key as Fedora font is too big and only allows 4 characters a box (when 5 are needed).
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: use the commands "rm -r "${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450"" and "WINEPREFIX="${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/" winetricks d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts" to get all games in one you have to use this command "WINEPREFIX="${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/" regedit" and then under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/Software/Wow6432Node/Microsoft/microsoft games copy pid and digitalproductid from age of empires 3 expansion pack 2/1.0 to age of empires 3 expansion pack/1.0 (i couldn't copy digitalproductid so i had to type the whole thing manually) and then when you launch Asian Dynastys all content will be present
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
Maxed out, getting 240fps+.
Didn't test MP.

Pop up a console before launching the game:

rm -r ${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450
WINEPREFIX=${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/ winetricks d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: Requires these tweaks to run: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/17#issuecomment-423592026. Consistently shuts off my PC after about ~5 minutes of playing
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: Needs `winetricks winxp mfc42 l3codecx sound=alsa`. I need to use alsa instead of pulseaudio, or the sound and parts of the game (scripting?) would hang up.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: The problem was that it did not accept CD Key. My solution was this: 'protontricks 105450 d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts' after running this game runs great. One more thing: after quitting the game, the game seems to be running in background as I can hear sound. Checked running processes and had to kill age3.exe to quit game completely.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti with Max-Q Design
notes: After following these steps (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/17#issuecomment-423592026) everything worked fine
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: Apply the following trick : https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/17#issuecomment-423592026 - once done the game works perfectly
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: After tweaking with "WINESERVER="${HOME}/.local/share/Steam/SteamApps/common/Proton 3.7/dist/bin/wineserver" WINETRICKS="${PWD}/winetricks" ${PWD}/protontricks 105450 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts", the game works flawlessly.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

cpu: AMD A10-8700P Radeon R6, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: So following the suggested fixes (WINESERVER="${HOME}/.steam/steamapps/common/Proton 3.16 Beta/dist/bin/wineserver" WINETRICKS="${PWD}/winetricks" ${PWD}/protontricks 105450 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts")its still not working.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
To get the game to work I entered in these commands into the terminal. 
(m -r "${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450")
(WINEPREFIX=${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/ winetricks d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts)

Overall the game runs well. I played for about 3 hours and did not experience any crashes or glitches.

notes:

title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes:
Works almost perfectly after using: WINEPREFIX=${HOME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/105450/pfx/ winetricks d3dx9 mfc42 winxp l3codecx corefonts 

But I noticed when I play the campaign that sometimes I have issues with the sound, e.g I only hear the dialogue sounds and not the normal sound from the game or vice versa. At one point in a cut scene the game even has hung up and I had to kill the game. Everything else works fine and flawlessly.
title: Age of Empires® III: Complete Collection

notes: The game doesn't respond to input for the first 10 or so seconds and can't build anything unless I restart steam, it like the alt key is being registered as pressed down. Audio glitches up but disabling music fixes it. Note that the audio problem is also on windows 10 for me so don't know if it's the game in general. Before running the stuff these other people suggest you need to install wine32(and possibly add 32bit architecture as well through terminal) if it's not already installed.
